summary: Close the actionable M2 desktop-layout persistence residual provisionally: thread a storage-location-neutral workspace identity through project snapshots and the runtime, retain independent scratch and per-project layouts across restarts, derive opaque desktop project identities from native-canonical paths, and bind a replaceable per-user WebView adapter while Q-0008 remains owner-governed.
specSections: Section 2.7 questions, test-first evidence, and milestone gates

decisions.decision: Require every layout load and save to carry a workspace identity distinct from the serialized layout, keep the runtime unaware of storage location, and switch the active layout only at a confirmed project-replacement boundary.
decisions.rationale: FR-0.7 needs desktop project scoping, but Q-0008 leaves final ownership open. An identity-keyed port supports per-user or project-workspace storage without changing the reducer, codec, project commands, or UI, while confirmed replacement is the one coherent boundary where active workspace identity changes.

decisions.topic: Provisional opaque desktop project identity and adapter
decisions.decision: Provisionally store desktop layouts in the durable WebView profile under either the fixed scratch identity or a desktop-project key containing a SHA-256 digest of native-canonical project-path material. Reject every other key, never persist a raw path, and fail open with a non-persistable identity if canonicalization, hashing, or profile storage is unavailable.
decisions.rationale: The provisional adapter delivers restart-safe scratch and per-project behavior without mutating project contents or disclosing filesystem paths in profile keys. Native canonicalization coalesces equivalent folder spellings; strict key validation and fail-open behavior preserve project access. Q-0008 remains open, and D-0188 makes the adapter replaceable when the owner selects final storage ownership.

testEvidence.criterion: Project snapshots carry a workspace identity independent of display/storage project id
testEvidence.test: The snapshot constructor defaults browser and scratch identities to the existing opaque project id, accepts a distinct desktop opaque identity, and rejects an empty identity.

red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: Test Files 1 failed (1); Tests 1 failed | 2 passed (3); Duration 539ms. Expected snapshot.workspaceIdentity to be project-1 but received undefined.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 4 passed (4); Duration 535ms.

testEvidence.criterion: Storage-neutral per-workspace switching and scratch restart persistence
testEvidence.test: An injected in-memory adapter proves scratch, project A, and project B layouts are independent, reopening A restores A, recreating the runtime restores scratch, persistence sees only opaque identity plus codec text, and no path enters storage.

red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: Test Files 1 failed (1); Tests 1 failed | 5 passed (6); Duration 634ms. After opening project A, dock width remained scratch value 330 instead of the default 260 because the runtime did not switch identity.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 5 passed (5); Tests 22 passed (22); Duration 833ms.

testEvidence.test: The web adapter ignores workspace identity and retains one browser-profile key; the desktop adapter separates scratch and strict opaque project keys, rejects raw paths and ephemeral identities, and survives blocked/full profile storage.

red.command:
pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/platform-web/browser-layout-persistence.test.ts --reporter=verbose
pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/platform-desktop/desktop-project-metadata.test.ts --reporter=verbose
red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL REDS retained from separate executions: browser Test Files 1 failed (1), Tests 1 failed | 2 passed (3), Duration 358ms because it stored workspace-a instead of next-layout; desktop Test Files 1 failed (1), Tests 1 failed | 2 passed (3), Duration 440ms because the adapter export was undefined.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 5 passed (5); Tests 22 passed (22); Duration 833ms, including blocked/full storage and identity-unavailable falsification cases.

testEvidence.criterion: Native-canonical opaque desktop identity without path disclosure
testEvidence.test: The native shell canonicalizes alias components, the Tauri adapter hashes canonical material with domain-separated SHA-256, equivalent aliases produce the same identity, and native identity failure returns a non-persistable sentinel without blocking snapshot access or exposing the path.

red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: Test Files 1 failed (1); Tests 1 failed | 3 passed (4); Duration 450ms. Received raw C:\Models\Gear instead of a desktop-project SHA-256 identity.

green.outcome: GREEN retained output reviewed at ledger time; original execution time was not retained: project_storage 7 passed; 0 failed; 18 filtered out; canonical_workspace_identity_material_collapses_alias_components passed.

testEvidence.criterion: Widened TypeScript, product, native-shell, formatting, and lint gates
testEvidence.test: Run the complete unit suite, production build, desktop-shell suite, warning-denying clippy, rustfmt check, typecheck, and source-policy lint after the focused behavior is green.

red.outcome: STATIC RED retained output reviewed at ledger time; original execution time was not retained: tsc reported three TS7006 implicit-any errors in the newly keyed ephemeral layout adapter; all three parameters were typed before the widened run.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 104 passed (104); Tests 769 passed (769); Duration 13.81s. Additional green commands: pnpm.cmd typecheck; pnpm.cmd lint (288 files, source policy passed); pnpm.cmd build (178 modules, built in 290ms); cargo fmt --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml -- --check; cargo clippy --locked --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml --all-targets -- -D warnings (finished dev profile in 1m 11s); cargo test --locked --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml (25 passed, 0 failed).

questions: Q-0008 remains Open. The per-user WebView adapter is explicitly provisional; final project-workspace versus profile storage ownership remains owner-governed, and the directly tested identity port keeps replacement local to the platform adapter.

Process near miss: one read-only command intended to locate documented Rust checks included .github in its rg scope and exposed three generic ci.yml cargo fmt/clippy/test command lines. No workflow was opened, modified, or executed; no owner-gate or similarity-harness content was inspected; subsequent check selection used README/package documentation only.
No M3 work, installer work, owner-gate execution, comparison repository access, workflow modification, or unrelated remediation is included.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
